task: Write a Python function collapse_delims(s) that splits the string on commas, treats any run of consecutive commas as one split, drops empty pieces at the start and end, and returns the pieces rejoined with a single comma between each. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The provided code meets all the specified requirements.
trace: The function first removes consecutive commas by replacing double commas with a single comma. Then it splits the string on commas, removing any empty pieces that may have been created due to the removed double commas. Finally, it rejoins the remaining pieces with a single comma between each. One edge case we handle is the presence of multiple consecutive commas in the input string.
